label: Network Topology
description: Arrangement of nodes of a computer network, viewed as a layout of a computer network.

label: Nodes
description: Components that need to be arranged to establish communication in a network.

label: Communication
description: The establishment of data exchange among all nodes in a network.

label: Physical Topology
description: The placement of various nodes in a network.

label: Logical Topology
description: How the data is going to flow in the network.

label: Bus Topology
description: A network topology where all data is transmitted over a common transmission medium and received by all nodes simultaneously.

label: Common Transmission Medium (Bus)
description: A single wire or cable in bus topology capable of sending and receiving data bidirectionally.

label: Advantages of Bus Topology
description: Benefits of bus topology, including less expense, suitability for temporary networks, and node failures not affecting others.

label: Disadvantages of Bus Topology
description: Drawbacks of bus topology, including not being fault tolerant, limited cable length, and no security.

label: Ring Topology
description: A bus topology arranged in a closed loop, where each node has two connections to its nearest neighbors, and communication is unidirectional.

label: Token (Ring Topology)
description: A mechanism in ring topology that dictates which node gets to send data, circulated throughout the network.

label: Advantages of Ring Topology
description: Benefits of ring topology, including better performance than bus topology and all nodes having equal access.

label: Disadvantages of Ring Topology
description: Drawbacks of ring topology, including unidirectional communication, single point of failure affecting the whole network, performance degradation with high load, and no security.

label: Star Topology
description: A network topology where every node is connected to a central node (hub or switch), and all traffic must pass through this central node.

label: Central Node (Hub/Switch)
description: A central device in a star topology that performs centralized administration and through which all network traffic passes.

label: Advantages of Star Topology
description: Benefits of star topology, including ease of design and implementation, centralized administration, and scalability.

label: Disadvantages of Star Topology
description: Drawbacks of star topology, including a single point of failure affecting the whole network, bottlenecks due to overloaded hub/switch, and increased cost.

label: Extended Star Topology
description: A variation of star topology where two or more star topologies are connected.

label: Mesh Topology
description: A network topology where each node is directly connected to every other node in the network, providing fault tolerance and reliability.

label: Advantages of Mesh Topology
description: Benefits of mesh topology, including being fault tolerant and reliable.

label: Disadvantages of Mesh Topology
description: Drawbacks of mesh topology, including issues with broadcasting messages, being expensive, and impractical for large networks.

label: Hybrid Topology
description: A network topology that combines two or more different topologies (e.g., bus, star, mesh).
